mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day equity returns in >55% of observations during the specified regime, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated consumer price index signals persistent inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPI exceeds the threshold, or signal loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates confirms a resilient labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of payroll growth and stable unemployment fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings capacity, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward equity returns (p > 0.10) over a 10-year backtest, or out-of-sample R-squared remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production signals an abrupt supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >50% of instances following a >1.0% industrial production drop, or the signal produces a negative Sharpe ratio in live forward testing.
